context.project_description: Implement token usage tracking for LLM queries by instrumenting provider calls using existing instrumentation hooks to see how many tokens each query costs.
key_requirements: Instrument LLM providers (chat, embed, rerank, vision) with token counting via BenchmarkHook
Augment responses with token counts when available from provider SDKs
Expose token usage metrics per query through a global tracker or API endpoint
constraints: Must leverage existing InstrumentedProxy and BenchmarkHook infrastructure in fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py
Cannot modify LLM provider interfaces directly - must wrap via instrumentation system
Token counting should integrate with existing logging framework rather than adding new dependencies
context.existing_context: fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py, fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py
stakeholders: system architect responsible for maintaining existing instrumentation infrastructure
developer tasked with implementing token usage tracking without breaking current architecture
end user who wants to see how many LLM tokens each query costs

in_scope: instrument LLM provider calls to track total tokens consumed per query using existing BenchmarkHook and InstrumentedProxy infrastructure
augment responses with token counts where provider SDKs support it
expose token usage via global state manager or logging output
out_of_scope: creating a new custom TokenUsageTracker class that wraps all LLM providers independently
implementing comprehensive cross-provider token tracking at method level
adding external token counting services beyond what's already available in provider SDKs
building API endpoints for querying usage history by session/query ID (optional extension)

existing_files: fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py — Instrumentation system for benchmarking plugin performance
fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py — Provider protocols for LLM clients
fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py — OpenAI provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py — Anthropic provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py — Cohere provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py — Ollama provider wrappers using direct HTTP calls
needed_artifacts: fitz_ai/llm/providers/token_tracker.py — new hook class that implements BenchmarkHook to capture token usage during LLM calls
fitz_ai/llm/providers/instrumentation.py — new factory function to wrap LLM provider methods with InstrumentedProxy

assumptions.assumption: The original request only asks to 'see how many LLM tokens each query costs', not track at method level or across all provider types
assumptions.impact: Implementing a full TokenUsageTracker class that wraps all providers would add unnecessary complexity and violate scope boundaries; the solution should focus on per-query tracking

assumptions.assumption: There is an existing instrumentation system (`InstrumentedProxy`, `BenchmarkHook`) available for wrapping components with hooks without modifying provider interfaces directly
assumptions.impact: If this infrastructure doesn't exist or isn't usable, we'd need to build custom tracking mechanisms per provider which would violate the principle of reusing existing patterns and increase maintenance burden

assumptions.assumption: LLM providers' SDKs provide built-in token usage information in their responses (e.g., OpenAI's response metadata)
assumptions.impact: If providers don't expose token counts internally, we'd have to implement custom counting logic based on model context length limits or use external libraries which adds complexity and potential inaccuracies

assumptions.assumption: The solution should integrate with existing logging systems rather than requiring new infrastructure like API endpoints for token usage queries
assumptions.impact: If we assume centralized logging is not sufficient, this could lead to building additional data stores or APIs which may be outside the scope of what was requested

assumptions.assumption: Query ID can be reliably tracked through the call stack and stored in a thread-local or global state for access by the tracker hook
assumptions.impact: If query IDs are not consistently available or properly propagated, tracking may break or become inconsistent across concurrent requests

approaches.name: Wrapper Injection Approach
approaches.description: Inject custom wrapper classes around LLM providers that track token usage internally while maintaining existing interfaces.
pros: Maintains backward compatibility with all public APIs
Can capture usage data from SDK responses where available
cons: Requires careful injection points throughout engine pipeline
Session state management needed for tracking across multiple calls
Only works when underlying providers support rich response objects
best_for: When breaking existing interfaces is not acceptable
Systems where token usage data must be captured without API changes

approaches.name: Interface Modification Approach
approaches.description: Modify core provider protocols to return structured responses containing usage metadata while preserving string returns for backward compatibility.
pros: Provides clean, direct access to token usage data
Clear separation of concerns between data and metadata
cons: Breaking change that affects all consumers
May require migration effort for existing users
best_for: When architectural clarity allows breaking changes
Systems where token tracking is a core requirement

architecture.reasoning: The Wrapper Injection Approach is recommended because it avoids breaking existing interfaces while still providing the capability to track tokens. The analysis clearly states that current provider methods return simple types (str, list[float]) without token metadata, and the instrumentation system operates on final results rather than SDK responses. Since we cannot modify core contracts due to breaking change constraints, a lightweight wrapper solution allows for session-based tracking without disrupting existing APIs. The Interface Modification Approach would provide cleaner integration but is ruled out by the requirement to maintain backward compatibility.

key_tradeoffs.interface_modification_vs_backward_compa: Modifying provider contracts to return structured data with usage info enables proper token tracking but breaks existing APIs; maintaining current interfaces requires workarounds that limit accuracy and visibility
key_tradeoffs.instrumentation_depth: Using InstrumentedProxy system provides broad coverage but cannot access SDK-level response metadata since providers already transform responses to strings; custom wrapping offers precise control but adds complexity

technology_considerations: OpenAI Python SDK for token usage extraction via .usage attribute on ChatCompletion objects
Dataclasses in Python for structuring Answer class with optional token_usage field
InstrumentedProxy system for method interception and tracing (limited by string-returning provider contracts)
Type hinting with TypedDict for defining structured response interfaces that include usage data
Custom wrapper classes like TrackedChatProvider to inject tracking without modifying original APIs
architecture.scope_statement: Implement custom instrumentation wrappers that track LLM token usage during query processing while maintaining all public interfaces unchanged, requiring careful injection of tracking logic into engine execution paths.

adrs.title: ADR: Track LLM Token Usage in Query Processing
adrs.context: The current architecture lacks mechanisms to capture and expose LLM token usage data from three critical points: LLM Provider Layer, Engine Pipeline, and API Exposure. All provider methods return raw strings without any token metadata, and Answer objects lack a way to carry token usage information.
adrs.decision: Implement lightweight session-based tracking utility injected into query processing contexts where LLM calls occur, maintaining backward compatibility by not modifying public interfaces. This approach requires careful refactoring of relevant engine logic to inject tracing during execution flow only when token tracking is enabled.
adrs.rationale: The core issue is that all current provider method contracts return simple types (str, list[float]) which do not expose metadata such as .usage containing token counts. The instrumentation system operates on final results and cannot access SDK-level responses where usage data resides. Modifying interfaces would break existing code but enable proper tracking.
consequences: Minimal disruption to current API contracts
Requires careful engine logic refactoring for injection points
Token tracking only available when explicitly enabled via session context
Maintains backward compatibility with existing provider implementations
alternatives_considered: Modify ChatProvider interface to return structured data containing usage fields - rejected because it would break existing implementations and require extensive refactoring across all providers
Extend InstrumentedProxy system to capture SDK responses - rejected because hooks only receive final result values (strings) not raw API response objects with .usage metadata
Add token_usage field directly to Answer class without tracking mechanism - rejected because no actual token data can be captured from current provider contracts

adrs.title: ADR: Session-based Token Tracking via Wrapper Injection
adrs.context: Implementing token tracking in a system where provider interfaces cannot be modified due to breaking change constraints, using wrapper injection approach that operates on final results rather than SDK responses.
adrs.decision: Adopt wrapper injection pattern with session-scoped token tracking while maintaining existing return types and avoiding interface modifications.
adrs.rationale: This approach enables token metadata collection without disrupting current contracts. However, it introduces several tradeoffs including data crossing boundaries in an opaque manner, accepting latency as a failure mode, and excluding real-time monitoring capabilities from v1 implementation.
consequences: Token tracking information becomes part of session state rather than direct return values, making debugging harder
Performance overhead introduced by wrapper layer affects API response times
V1 excludes live metrics dashboard capability which would require more invasive instrumentation changes
alternatives_considered: Modify existing interfaces to include token metadata in return types - breaks backward compatibility and requires extensive coordination
Implement centralized telemetry service that intercepts all calls before they reach providers - introduces single point of failure
Use AOP-style decorators on all provider methods - increases complexity with multiple injection points

components.name: FitzKragEngine
responsibilities: Manage cloud cache for query results
interfaces: _store_cloud_cache(query_text: str, addresses: list, answer: Answer) -> None

components.name: fitz
components.purpose: Stateful SDK for the Fitz KRAG framework
responsibilities: Initialize with collection and config path
Point to source directories for ingestion
interfaces: __init__(collection: str, config_path: Optional[Union[str, Path]], auto_init: bool) -> None
ask(question: str, top_k: Optional[int], conversation_context: Optional['ConversationContext']) -> Answer

components.name: RerankProvider
responsibilities: Rank documents based on query relevance
interfaces: rerank(query: str, documents: list[str], top_n: int | None) -> list[RerankResult]

components.name: VisionProvider
interfaces: describe_image(image_base64: str, prompt: str | None) -> str

artifacts.content:
# Copyright (c) 2024 Fitz AI
"""
Answer - paradigm-agnostic answer representation. See docs/API_REFERENCE.md for examples.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Optional


timeout = 30

@dataclass
class Answer:
    text: str
    provenance: list[str]
    token_usage: Optional[int] = None  # NEW FIELD

    def __post_init__(self):
        if self.token_usage is not None and not isinstance(self.token_usage, int):
            raise ValueError("token_usage must be an integer or None")
artifacts.purpose: Update Answer class to include token usage tracking field

artifacts.content:
# Copyright (c) 2024 Fitz AI
"""
Answer - paradigm-agnostic answer representation. See docs/API_REFERENCE.md for examples.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Optional


timeout = 30

@dataclass
class Answer:
    text: str
    provenance: list[str]
    token_usage: Optional[int] = None  # NEW FIELD

    def __post_init__(self):
        if self.token_usage is not None and not isinstance(self.token_usage, int):
            raise ValueError("token_usage must be an integer or None")
artifacts.purpose: Update Answer class to include token usage tracking field

phases.name: Read Before Writing
phases.objective: Verify integration points by reading source
phases.verification_command: pytest fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_base.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_openai.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_anthropic.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_cohere.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_ollama.py

phases.name: Create Token Tracker Hook
phases.objective: Implement a BenchmarkHook that tracks total tokens consumed per session/query using thread-local storage for isolation
deliverables: fitz_ai/llm/providers/token_tracker.py file with TokenTracker class and global instance
key_risks: Thread safety issues with threading.local() in concurrent environments
Instrumentation system may not provide access to SDK response token data
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.providers.token_tracker import TokenTracker; print('Token tracker loaded successfully')"

phases.name: Create Instrumentation Factory
phases.objective: Build a factory function that wraps LLM providers with instrumentation to track calls through the existing BenchmarkHook system
key_risks: Provider interface contract violations from wrapping mechanism
InstrumentedProxy._wrap_method() edge cases breaking existing functionality
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.providers.instrumentation import instrument_provider; print('Instrumentation factory loaded successfully')"

phases.name: Integrate with FitzKragEngine
phases.objective: Modify engine to use token tracking during query processing by resetting session tokens and instrumenting providers
deliverables: Modified fitz_ai/engines/fitz_krag/engine.py with token tracker integration in answer() and _init_components() methods
key_risks: Instrumentation not properly applied due to configuration errors causing silent failure
Token tracking not triggered if instrumentation isn't working correctly

phases.name: Expose Token Usage in Answers
phases.objective: Extend Answer dataclass to support token usage metadata and provide accessors for tracking consumption
deliverables: Updated fitz_ai/core/answer.py with token_usage property and add_token_usage method
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.core.answer import Answer; a = Answer('test'); print('Answer token usage access works')"

risks.description: The BenchmarkHook system may not be able to track token usage at the right level since current instrumentation only operates on final results and doesn't access SDK metadata like .usage fields.
risks.mitigation: Implement comprehensive testing to verify that hook system functions as expected and validate actual token tracking behavior with real provider calls.
risks.contingency: Refactor approach using direct provider wrapping instead of instrumentation-based tracking if SDK metadata access proves impossible.
risks.verification: Run python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.providers.token_tracker import TokenTracker; print('Loaded')" and test actual provider call behavior.

risks.description: OpenAI/Anthropic/Cohere SDKs may not provide token usage in response metadata, making it impossible to track actual tokens consumed per query.
risks.mitigation: Implement fallback mechanisms and document limitations clearly for users of this feature.
risks.contingency: Develop custom counting logic or use SDK-specific token tracking when available in future versions.
risks.verification: Test a provider call directly and check if response contains usage data fields.

risks.description: Thread-local storage may cause race conditions in multi-threaded environments when tracking token sessions across concurrent queries.
risks.mitigation: Add thorough unit tests that simulate multiple concurrent threads and verify isolated token counting per thread context.
risks.contingency: Implement a more robust synchronization mechanism if thread safety issues are detected during testing.
risks.verification: Run concurrent unit tests with multiple threads making provider calls to confirm session isolation.

risks.description: Provider interface contract violations could occur due to wrapping mechanism interfering with existing method signatures or behavior.
risks.mitigation: Conduct extensive integration testing across all provider types and verify no regression in functionality.
risks.contingency: Modify instrumentation approach to preserve exact function signatures and return type compatibility for providers.
risks.verification: Execute unit tests on instrumented providers ensuring identical behavior before/after wrapping.

risks.description: External SDK behavior changes (like adding .usage fields) may cause mismatch between our assumptions and reality.
risks.mitigation: Design the system with extensibility in mind to easily adapt when new token metadata becomes available.
risks.contingency: Implement dynamic detection of SDK response formats and adjust tracking logic accordingly.
risks.verification: Monitor SDK releases for any changes to usage field availability or response structure.

risks.description: Instrumentation system bugs in maybe_wrap() or InstrumentedProxy._wrap_method() could silently break existing functionality.
risks.mitigation: Create comprehensive unit tests covering edge cases of the instrumentation wrapper behavior and add logging for debugging failures.
risks.contingency: Implement error recovery and graceful degradation in case wrapped methods fail during execution.
risks.verification: Verify that all provider methods work correctly after being instrumented by testing their invocation.

recommended_contingencies: Refactor approach using direct provider wrapping instead of instrumentation-based tracking if SDK metadata access proves impossible
Implement fallback mechanisms and document limitations clearly for users of this feature
Add thorough unit tests that simulate multiple concurrent threads to confirm session isolation
Conduct extensive integration testing across all provider types to ensure no regression in functionality

phases.verification_command: pytest fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_base.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_openai.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_anthropic.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_cohere.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_ollama.py
